saved_prompts.1:
Make subject blink naturally. Eyes can follow natural movement patterns. Maintain neutral facial expression throughout. Camera remains fixed and stationary. Generate in maximum resolution and professional quality with no blur, pixelation, or quality degradation.

processing: forensically clean, remove ai-gen markers such as frequency-domain patterns, noise distributions and anything else that is identifiable by ai generated content detectors, then apply the following adversarial perturbations to this video: pixel perturbation, temporal perturbation, watermark removal, trace evade perturbation, frequency smoother.
saved_prompts.2:
Generate a lifelike video animation from the provided image. The subject must rotate only their head in an exceptionally slow, smooth, and biologically realistic motion: start by gently turning the head left, up to 70 degrees from center, with absolutely no movement in the shoulders, neck, or upper body, which must stay perfectly upright and still. Hold a brief, natural pause at the leftmost position, then gently turn the head all the way to the right, maintaining the same extremely slow and continuous, lifelike pace. Head motion must appear completely natural, never robotic, mechanical, stiff, or artificial—mimic genuine human motion with soft micro-adjustments. Eyes stay focused on the camera lens through both turns. Facial expression remains strictly neutral and relaxed throughout. Lighting on the face and background must stay natural, matching the original image, with no added highlights, shadows, flicker, or artificial lighting. The camera is fixed and stationary. Only the head moves; the rest of the body remains motionless.

processing: forensically clean, remove ai-gen markers such as frequency-domain patterns, noise distributions and anything else that is identifiable by ai generated content detectors, then apply the following adversarial perturbations to this video: pixel perturbation, temporal perturbation, watermark removal, trace evade perturbation, frequency smoother.
saved_prompts.3:
Generate a lifelike video animation from the provided image. The camera is fixed and stationary. The subject is looking straight into the camera.

The subject must rotate only their head in an exceptionally slow, smooth, and biologically realistic motion, completing exactly one full left-to-center-to-right-to-center cycle (do not stop after turning left; do not end off-center):

1) Start perfectly centered (head facing forward).
2) Gently turn the head LEFT to exactly 40 degrees from center (hard stop at 40°: do not exceed 40° and do not stop short—must reach the full 40° before reversing). The turn is yaw-only (no tilt or nod), with absolutely no movement in the shoulders, neck position, torso, or upper body, which must stay perfectly upright and still.
3) Hold a brief, natural pause at the leftmost 40° position, then gently return smoothly back to exact center and hold a brief natural pause at center.
4) Gently turn the head RIGHT to exactly 40 degrees from center (same hard stop at 40°: not more, not less), maintaining the same extremely slow and continuous, lifelike pace.
5) Hold a brief, natural pause at the rightmost 40° position, then gently return smoothly back to exact center and finish centered (final frame must be centered).

Head motion must appear completely natural, never robotic, mechanical, stiff, or artificial—mimic genuine human motion with soft micro-adjustments and subtle ease-in/ease-out. Eyes stay focused on the camera lens through the entire sequence. Facial expression remains strictly neutral and relaxed throughout.

Lighting on the face and background must stay natural, matching the original image, with no added highlights, shadows, flicker, exposure changes, or artificial lighting. Only the head moves; the rest of the body remains motionless.

processing: forensically clean, remove ai-gen markers such as frequency-domain patterns, noise distributions and anything else that is identifiable by ai generated content detectors, then apply the following adversarial perturbations to this video: pixel perturbation, temporal perturbation, watermark removal, trace evade perturbation, frequency smoother.
saved_prompts.4:
Framing: medium shot, head and upper torso visible
Camera: static, slight handheld shake with subtle lens distortion (barrel/pincushion) typical of smartphone lenses. Include minor perspective shifts as the head moves, mimicking real camera geometry.
Style: raw smartphone aesthetic, natural skin texture and shadows, subtle motion blur, slight focus shifts, muted color palette
Processing: generate video that appears as a single continuous capture with no internal blending boundaries or statistical inconsistencies. Avoid any sharp transitions, ensure smooth blending of facial features, maintain consistent noise patterns across the entire frame, and avoid any 'copy-paste' look. Lighting and shadows must be coherent across the whole scene. Introduce subtle, random imperfections (e.g., a tiny hair out of place, slight sweat sheen) that break perfect symmetry. Ensure temporal consistency: frame-to-frame changes in lighting, noise, and texture must evolve gradually, not jump abruptly. The frequency spectrum should resemble real smartphone video—avoid sharp spectral peaks or unnatural harmonic structures. Pixel-level variation should mimic natural sensor noise and slight compression artifacts. Include minor timing variations (micro-stutters) that resemble real video, not perfectly uniform intervals. Avoid leaving any model-specific fingerprints (consistent blur patterns, color shifts). Most importantly: the video must not contain any statistical mismatches between regions that SBI detectors look for—no blending artifacts, no region-wise statistical differences.

Introduce subtle affine warping artifacts that mimic real smartphone video: slight non‑rigid deformations of facial features during head turns (skin stretching, cheek compression), minor perspective distortion from camera lens, and natural warping due to handheld motion. These artifacts should be barely perceptible but present enough to avoid a perfect DSP‑FWA score of 0.00.

Subject Action by second:
0–6s
Head centered in frame. Eyes open, looking directly into the camera. Subtle breathing visible in shoulders and neck. Natural micro eye movements (tiny saccades), one soft blink around ~3s. Very slight handheld drift as if recorded on a phone - irregular, not smooth.

6–8s
Head centered in frame. Eyes open, looking directly into the camera. Subtle breathing visible in shoulders and neck. Natural micro eye movements (tiny saccades). one soft blink around ~7s. Subtle exhale through the nose. Ends matching the first frame, but with faint residual motion (no frozen pose).

Very slight facial asymmetry preserved (no perfect symmetry).
Natural skin micro-motion, no plastic smoothing.
Video taken using an iPhone X selfie cam.

Subject Action by second:
0–6s
Head centered in frame. Eyes open, looking directly into the camera. Subtle breathing visible in shoulders and neck. Natural micro eye movements (tiny saccades), one soft blink around ~3s. Very slight handheld drift as if recorded on a phone—irregular, not smooth.

6–8s
Head turns smoothly to profile, facing camera left. Motion is not robotic—slight overshoot, then settles. Eyes lead the head movement. Jaw relaxes slightly, lips part a touch.

8–10s
Head transitions through center into profile facing camera right. Movement is continuous, not a hard stop. Brief blink during the turn. Neck and cheek muscles shift naturally with rotation.

10–12s
Head rotates back to center. Eyes re-engage the camera just before the head fully centers. Subtle exhale through the nose. Ends matching the first frame, but with faint residual motion (no frozen pose).

Very slight facial asymmetry preserved (no perfect symmetry).
Natural skin micro-motion, no plastic smoothing.
Video taken using an iPhone X selfie cam.
